findings.title: Weak Content-Security-Policy Configuration
findings.observation: The Content-Security-Policy contains unsafe-inline or wildcard directives, which weakens script source guarantees.
findings.aiAnalysis: The CSP contains directives like unsafe-inline or wildcard hosts. While a policy is present, these directives bypass some of the primary XSS mitigations.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: Unsafe-inline or wildcard values are sometimes required for legacy script compatibility or CDN hosting. This is reported as a hardening recommendation. [Validation Review: Exploitability not demonstrated. Downgraded to Best Practice.]
findings.businessImpact: Increases the risk of XSS execution if a parameter reflection vulnerability is introduced.
findings.remediation: Refactor client-side code to use event listeners instead of inline scripts, and remove unsafe-inline and wildcard source hosts from CSP.

findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy contains unsafe-inline or wildcard directives, which weakens script source guarantees.
findings.reasoning: The CSP contains directives like unsafe-inline or wildcard hosts. While a policy is present, these directives bypass some of the primary XSS mitigations.

findings.title: Strict-Transport-Security Preloaded (Mitigated)
findings.observation: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is missing on the raw response, but the domain is preloaded in browsers.
findings.evidence: Domain is in HSTS Preload registry
findings.detectionLogic: Match hostname against HSTS Preload registry
findings.aiAnalysis: No HSTS header was observed. However, the target is a known enterprise domain preloaded in modern browsers via HSTS Preload lists. Therefore, the lack of an explicit HSTS header on the response does not present an exploitable risk.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: HSTS preload lists are built directly into modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari), which force HTTPS immediately before any connection. This fully mitigates the risk. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: None. Browser security enforcement prevents any plain-text transmission to this domain.
findings.remediation: No remediation required. The domain is fully secured via the HSTS Preload registry.

findings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is missing on the raw response, but the domain is preloaded in browsers.
findings.reasoning: No HSTS header was observed. However, the target is a known enterprise domain preloaded in modern browsers via HSTS Preload lists. Therefore, the lack of an explicit HSTS header on the response does not present an exploitable risk.

findings.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
findings.observation: The Referrer-Policy header is missing, which could leak sensitive parameters or tokens in referrer headers to external hosts.
findings.aiAnalysis: No Referrer-Policy header was observed. The browser's default behavior applies, which might send URL paths including query strings to third parties.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: If the site does not contain sensitive tokens or IDs in URL paths or parameters, the risk of data leakage is negligible. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: Potential leakage of sensitive parameters or tokens to external sites through HTTP Referer headers.

findings.description: Accessing /api/swagger returned HTTP status 200. This path exposes sensitive configurations, database files, or administrative modules.

findings.title: Exposed Sensitive Path: /api/docs
findings.observation: Accessing /api/docs returned HTTP status 200. This path exposes sensitive configurations, database files, or administrative modules.
findings.evidence: URL: https://www.youtube.com/api/docs | Status: 200 | Proof: Status code matched and differs from 404 baseline
findings.detectionLogic: Audit HTTP status code and match response body signatures against 404 baselines
findings.aiAnalysis: The path returned success and differed from the custom 404 baseline. However, no specific file signature was matched in the response body.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: The response differs from the baseline, suggesting a custom route or potential directory disclosure. Since no structural credentials were confirmed, the risk confidence is lower. [Validation Review: Exploitability not demonstrated. Downgraded to Best Practice.]
findings.businessImpact: Potential disclosure of administrative endpoints or swagger documentation, aiding attacker reconnaissance.
findings.remediation: Configure your web server to return a 404 or 403 status code for this directory, or delete the file from the production server.

findings.description: Accessing /api/docs returned HTTP status 200. This path exposes sensitive configurations, database files, or administrative modules.
findings.reasoning: The path returned success and differed from the custom 404 baseline. However, no specific file signature was matched in the response body.

findings.title: Reflected XSS Checks Passed
findings.observation: No unescaped reflection of scripting payloads was detected in the tested input fields.

findings.remediation: Ensure strict contextual output escaping is maintained.

findings.aiAnalysis: The tested application did not leak any database exceptions or errors.

findings.remediation: Continue enforcing SQL parameterization across all query operations.

findings.aiAnalysis: Redirect requests were either ignored or did not point to the external test domain.

findings.title: No Session Cookies Set
findings.observation: The target endpoint did not issue any Set-Cookie headers in the audited response.
findings.aiAnalysis: The application does not set cookies in its HTTP response, eliminating the risk of insecure cookie parameters.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: Confirm that cookies are indeed not used. APIs using authorization tokens do not set cookies. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.remediation: Ensure cookies continue to be avoided if session tokens are handled via custom headers.

findings.description: The web server exposes its software type in the response headers.
findings.reasoning: Only the general server software name is disclosed, which is standard configuration disclosure.

allFindings.title: Weak Content-Security-Policy Configuration
allFindings.observation: The Content-Security-Policy contains unsafe-inline or wildcard directives, which weakens script source guarantees.
allFindings.aiAnalysis: The CSP contains directives like unsafe-inline or wildcard hosts. While a policy is present, these directives bypass some of the primary XSS mitigations.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Unsafe-inline or wildcard values are sometimes required for legacy script compatibility or CDN hosting. This is reported as a hardening recommendation. [Validation Review: Exploitability not demonstrated. Downgraded to Best Practice.]
allFindings.businessImpact: Increases the risk of XSS execution if a parameter reflection vulnerability is introduced.
allFindings.remediation: Refactor client-side code to use event listeners instead of inline scripts, and remove unsafe-inline and wildcard source hosts from CSP.

allFindings.description: The Content-Security-Policy contains unsafe-inline or wildcard directives, which weakens script source guarantees.
allFindings.reasoning: The CSP contains directives like unsafe-inline or wildcard hosts. While a policy is present, these directives bypass some of the primary XSS mitigations.

allFindings.title: Strict-Transport-Security Preloaded (Mitigated)
allFindings.observation: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is missing on the raw response, but the domain is preloaded in browsers.
allFindings.evidence: Domain is in HSTS Preload registry
allFindings.detectionLogic: Match hostname against HSTS Preload registry
allFindings.aiAnalysis: No HSTS header was observed. However, the target is a known enterprise domain preloaded in modern browsers via HSTS Preload lists. Therefore, the lack of an explicit HSTS header on the response does not present an exploitable risk.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: HSTS preload lists are built directly into modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari), which force HTTPS immediately before any connection. This fully mitigates the risk. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.businessImpact: None. Browser security enforcement prevents any plain-text transmission to this domain.
allFindings.remediation: No remediation required. The domain is fully secured via the HSTS Preload registry.

allFindings.description: The Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header is missing on the raw response, but the domain is preloaded in browsers.
allFindings.reasoning: No HSTS header was observed. However, the target is a known enterprise domain preloaded in modern browsers via HSTS Preload lists. Therefore, the lack of an explicit HSTS header on the response does not present an exploitable risk.

allFindings.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
allFindings.observation: The Referrer-Policy header is missing, which could leak sensitive parameters or tokens in referrer headers to external hosts.
allFindings.aiAnalysis: No Referrer-Policy header was observed. The browser's default behavior applies, which might send URL paths including query strings to third parties.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: If the site does not contain sensitive tokens or IDs in URL paths or parameters, the risk of data leakage is negligible. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.businessImpact: Potential leakage of sensitive parameters or tokens to external sites through HTTP Referer headers.

allFindings.description: HTTPS is enforced, HTTP redirects to HTTPS, and HSTS is configured with a high max-age.
allFindings.reasoning: Traffic encryption and enforcement configurations meet security standards.

allFindings.title: Exposed Sensitive Path: /backup
allFindings.observation: Accessing /backup returned HTTP status 200. This path exposes sensitive configurations, database files, or administrative modules.
allFindings.evidence: URL: https://www.youtube.com/backup | Status: 200 | Proof: Status code matched and differs from 404 baseline
allFindings.detectionLogic: Audit HTTP status code and match response body signatures against 404 baselines
allFindings.aiAnalysis: The path returned success and differed from the custom 404 baseline. However, no specific file signature was matched in the response body.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: The response differs from the baseline, suggesting a custom route or potential directory disclosure. Since no structural credentials were confirmed, the risk confidence is lower. [Validation Review: Exploitability not demonstrated. Downgraded to Best Practice.]
allFindings.businessImpact: Potential disclosure of administrative endpoints or swagger documentation, aiding attacker reconnaissance.
allFindings.remediation: Configure your web server to return a 404 or 403 status code for this directory, or delete the file from the production server.

allFindings.description: Accessing /api/docs returned HTTP status 200. This path exposes sensitive configurations, database files, or administrative modules.
allFindings.reasoning: The path returned success and differed from the custom 404 baseline. However, no specific file signature was matched in the response body.

allFindings.title: Reflected XSS Checks Passed
allFindings.observation: No unescaped reflection of scripting payloads was detected in the tested input fields.
allFindings.evidence: Probed parameters q, search, name, input with standard XSS script alert.
allFindings.detectionLogic: Ensure parameter reflections are either absent or properly escaped
allFindings.aiAnalysis: Tested input parameters did not reflect raw scripting tags.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Input reflections are properly encoded, or variables are ignored by the application backend. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.remediation: Ensure strict contextual output escaping is maintained.

allFindings.reasoning: Tested input parameters did not reflect raw scripting tags.

allFindings.title: SQL Injection Checks Passed
allFindings.observation: No database query errors or SQL syntax leaks were detected in response to dynamic probes.

allFindings.detectionLogic: No database errors or WAF blocks triggered
allFindings.aiAnalysis: The tested application did not leak any database exceptions or errors.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Parameters successfully parsed without triggering database parser exceptions. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.remediation: Continue enforcing SQL parameterization across all query operations.
allFindings.description: No database query errors or SQL syntax leaks were detected in response to dynamic probes.
allFindings.reasoning: The tested application did not leak any database exceptions or errors.

allFindings.title: Open Redirect Checks Passed
allFindings.observation: No arbitrary redirection to external evil domains was detected during parameters probing.
allFindings.evidence: Tested 8 typical redirect parameters with external domain payload.
allFindings.detectionLogic: Confirm Location header is either absent or stays within the origin domain
allFindings.aiAnalysis: Redirect requests were either ignored or did not point to the external test domain.

allFindings.reasoning: Redirect requests were either ignored or did not point to the external test domain.

allFindings.title: CORS Security Check Passed
allFindings.observation: No wildcard ACAO, origin echoing, or credentials leakage was detected.
allFindings.evidence: Tested normal request and requests with Origin header overrides.

allFindings.title: No Session Cookies Set
allFindings.observation: The target endpoint did not issue any Set-Cookie headers in the audited response.
allFindings.aiAnalysis: The application does not set cookies in its HTTP response, eliminating the risk of insecure cookie parameters.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Confirm that cookies are indeed not used. APIs using authorization tokens do not set cookies. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.remediation: Ensure cookies continue to be avoided if session tokens are handled via custom headers.
